notes: George Duke Soul Treasures is a Kontakt library built from samples of the personal instrument collection of jazz fusion legend George Duke, capturing a range of vintage keys and instruments used in his recordings across the 1970s and 1980s. The collection includes Fender Rhodes electric piano variants, Wurlitzer electric piano, Clavinet, Hammond-style organs, and vintage analog synthesizers — instruments central to the jazz fusion, funk, R&B, and soul genres Duke mastered. The character is decidedly vintage, with the warmth, slight imperfection, and analog tonal quality of well-used 1970s instruments. CC 11 (Expression) is the primary dynamic control and is especially effective on the Rhodes and Wurlitzer presets where dynamics are a core expressive element. CC 1 (Modulation) controls tremolo depth on applicable instruments — the slow, warm tremolo of the Rhodes or Wurlitzer is a defining vintage texture. The Pickup/Tone Character QC fundamentally alters the tonal flavour of each instrument, from bright and cutting to warm and rounded.

Volume.description: Master output level of the instrument

Expression.description: Dynamic expression — primary level control; critical for dynamic Rhodes/Wurlitzer playing expression

Modulation.description: Tremolo Depth — mod wheel adds vintage tremolo effect on applicable instruments like Rhodes and Wurlitzer

Sustain.description: Sustain pedal — extends note decay; shorter on electric pianos compared to acoustic grands

Release Time.description: Release time — controls how long instrument tones ring after key release

Attack Time.description: Attack envelope — shapes the onset from immediate to slightly softened

Brightness.description: Tonal brightness — shapes the high-frequency character across all source instruments

Reverb Send.description: Reverb wet level — adds space to the vintage instruments for studio placement

Quick Control 1.description: Pickup / Tone Character — adjusts the tonal character; simulates different pickup positions or tone settings on the source instrument

Quick Control 2.description: Amp / DI Blend — blends between the direct instrument signal and an amplified, slightly saturated tone

Quick Control 3.description: Tremolo Rate — speed of the vintage tremolo effect; from slow, warm wobble to fast shimmer

Quick Control 4.description: Tremolo Depth — intensity of the tremolo modulation; increase for more pronounced vintage tremolo effect

Quick Control 5.description: Reverb — onboard reverb depth within the Kontakt patch

Quick Control 6.description: Overdrive — amount of analog overdrive or preamp saturation for vintage grit and warmth

Quick Control 7.description: Release Noise — mechanical noise at key release; adds tactile realism to electric piano and organ pads

Quick Control 8.description: Humanise — subtle random velocity and timing variation for a more organic, lived-in vintage feel
